sample_requests: Annotate a clustered AnnData object with OmicVerse
Convert the OmicVerse annotation notebook into a reusable local skill

smoke_commands.name: synthetic-annotation-smoke
smoke_commands.command:
${PYTHON} - <<'PY'
from __future__ import annotations

import inspect
import json
import os

import anndata as ad
import numpy as np
import pandas as pd

from omicverse.single._annotation import Annotation
from omicverse.single._gptcelltype import gptcelltype
from omicverse.single._anno import pySCSA

os.environ["AGI_API_KEY"] = ""

adata = ad.AnnData(X=np.array([[1.0, 2.0], [3.0, 4.0]], dtype=float))
adata.obs["leiden"] = pd.Categorical(["0", "1"])

anno = Annotation(adata)
prompt = gptcelltype(
    {"0": ["MS4A1", "CD79A"], "1": ["NKG7", "PRF1"]},
    tissuename="PBMC",
    speciename="human",
    provider="openai",
    model="gpt-5-mini",
)
scsa = pySCSA(adata, model_path="temp/pySCSA_2024_v1_plus.db")

summary = {
    "annotation_sig": str(inspect.signature(Annotation.annotate)),
    "query_sig": str(inspect.signature(Annotation.query_reference)),
    "gpt_sig": str(inspect.signature(gptcelltype)),
    "scsa_sig": str(inspect.signature(pySCSA)),
    "annotation_init": hasattr(anno, "adata"),
    "prompt_is_str": isinstance(prompt, str),
    "prompt_mentions_pbmc": "PBMC" in prompt,
    "prompt_mentions_human": "human" in prompt.lower(),
    "scsa_has_cell_anno": hasattr(scsa, "cell_anno"),
}

print(json.dumps(summary, sort_keys=True))
PY
